DESCRIPTION: The Bidding Law regulates the acquisition of goods and services by the public sector, promoting economy and transparency.
FUNDAMENTAL PRINCIPLES: Publicity, equality, morality, efficiency, impartiality, and legality.
TYPES OF BIDDING: Open bidding, price quotation, invitation to bid, electronic auction, and public auction.
PHASES OF THE BIDDING PROCESS: Planning, publication of the notice, receipt of proposals, judgment, award, and approval.
JUDGMENT CRITERIA: Lowest price, best technique, technique and price, highest bid, and highest economic return.
SANCTIONS AND PENALTIES: Warning, fines, temporary suspension, prohibition from contracting, and declaration of ineligibility.
